_note: STATUS-CONTROL TEST (G2, T-C, OUTPUT-control: write the status RESULT onto the target). Offline RE: effective status byte +0x61 = (+0x1EF & 0xF2) | +0x57, recomputed in REAL code at 0x30D42A; engine tests +0x61 everywhere. The infliction roll is VM but the status BYTES are data the VM reads, so OR-ing a mask onto +0x1EF (master) AND +0x61 (mirror) should force the status. This profile forces CONTROL-FLIP (0x10) on RAMZA ONLY (TargetCharId=1). Chose 0x10 because it SURVIVES the per-turn recompute (0xF2 keeps bit 0x10), so the test is reliable (0x08 can't-act gets cleared each turn-tick and would rely on poll timing). The status sweep runs with a charId filter so ONLY Ramza is affected even if untracked - the rest of the party is untouched. RUN: relaunch the game, enter a battle with Ramza. OBSERVE: on Ramza's turn, does he act under ENEMY AI - i.e. turn hostile / attack his own party / move on his own (Charm/Confusion-style)? And/or a status icon on him? PASS = Ramza is clearly no longer under your control = writing +0x1EF/+0x61 forces a status (closes the biggest DCL gap; status is OUTPUT-controllable). Log shows [STATUS-OVERRIDE id=0x01 ... or(61=10 1EF=10)]. SAFE: only Ramza, struct writes only; just observe one of his turns, then flee/reset the battle. If you'd rather a calmer signal, tell me and I switch to 0x08 (turn skipped / can't act).
